content:
<USER_REQUEST>
You are acting as the Principal Software Architect, Principal Backend Engineer, Principal Frontend Engineer, DevSecOps Engineer, Site Reliability Engineer (SRE), Security Engineer, QA Lead, Database Architect, and Release Manager for the SHIFTLY platform.

The repository is feature complete.

Your objective is NOT to build new business features.

Your objective is to transform the existing codebase into a Production Release Candidate 2 (RC2) that is ready for enterprise deployment.

==========================================================================
MANDATORY ENGINEERING PRINCIPLES
==========================================================================

These principles override every other instruction.

1. Never solve problems by:
   - disabling ESLint rules
   - weakening TypeScript strict mode
   - lowering compiler strictness
   - suppressing errors globally
   - commenting out code
   - removing tests
   - bypassing validation
   - bypassing authentication
   - bypassing authorization
   - reducing security
   - ignoring runtime failures

2. Always fix the ROOT CAUSE.

3. Never regenerate working modules.

4. Never redesign the architecture.

5. Never recreate folders.

6. Never replace working implementations.

7. Modify ONLY files that genuinely require changes.

8. Preserve backwards compatibility unless a critical defect requires change.

9. Every modification must improve at least one of:

   - correctness
   - maintainability
   - readability
   - scalability
   - security
   - performance
   - reliability

10. If a lint rule must be locally suppressed,
    explain why,
    keep the suppression minimal,
    and include it in the final report.

11. Never claim:

- Build Passed
- Tests Passed
- Migration Successful
- Docker Running
- PostgreSQL Connected
- Redis Connected
- Kafka Connected
- OpenSearch Connected
- Playwright Passed

unless those actions were ACTUALLY EXECUTED successfully.

If execution cannot be performed because a dependency or environment is unavailable, explicitly report:

"NOT VERIFIED"

Never fabricate successful validation.

==========================================================================
PHASE 1 — COMPLETE REPOSITORY AUDIT
==========================================================================

Inspect the ENTIRE repository.

Review every:

Backend Module

Frontend Feature

React Component

React Hook

Redux Slice

TanStack Query Hook

DTO

Controller

Service

Repository

Guard

Interceptor

Middleware

Validation Pipe

Exception Filter

Event

Listener

WebSocket Gateway

Prisma Model

Migration

Seed

Dockerfile

Docker Compose

GitHub Action

Kubernetes Manifest

Terraform

Environment Configuration

Scripts

Build Configuration

Dependencies

Documentation

==========================================================================
PHASE 2 — ARCHITECTURE REVIEW
==========================================================================

Verify:

Clean Architecture

SOLID Principles

DRY

KISS

Separation of Concerns

Dependency Direction

Circular Dependencies

Code Duplication

Dead Code

Module Boundaries

Shared Package Usage

Repository Organization

Refactor ONLY where necessary.

==========================================================================
PHASE 3 — TYPESCRIPT QUALITY
==========================================================================

Review:

tsconfig

ESLint

Prettier

Restore important TypeScript safety rules wherever possible.

Specifically verify:

@typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises

@typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises

@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment

@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access

@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-call

@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-return

Do NOT globally disable these rules simply to achieve a green build.

Fix the implementation instead.

==========================================================================
PHASE 4 — BACKEND AUDIT
==========================================================================

Review every backend module.

Verify:

Controllers

Services

Repositories

DTO Validation

Transactions

Dependency Injection

Exception Handling

Logging

Audit Logging

Redis

Kafka

OpenSearch

AWS Integrations

Cron Jobs

Background Jobs

Retry Policies

Idempotency

Timeout Handling

Graceful Shutdown

Health Checks

Request Validation

Response Consistency

Query Optimization

Pagination

N+1 Prevention

Memory Usage

Connection Pooling

Security

Authorization

==========================================================================
PHASE 5 — AUTHENTICATION & SECURITY
==========================================================================

Review the complete authentication flow.

Verify:

JWT

Access Tokens

Refresh Tokens

Refresh Token Rotation

Refresh Token Hashing

Reuse Detection

Session Revocation

Device Sessions

OTP

OTP Expiration

OTP Lockout

Password Hashing

Role-Based Access Control

Permission Checks

Input Validation

Rate Limiting

Helmet

CORS

Content Security Policy

CSRF Protection (if applicable)

XSS Protection

SQL Injection Prevention

File Upload Validation

Secrets Management

Audit Logs

Correlation IDs

Request IDs

==========================================================================
PHASE 6 — DATABASE REVIEW
==========================================================================

Review Prisma.

Verify:

Models

Relations

Indexes

Composite Indexes

Unique Constraints

Foreign Keys

Cascade Rules

Transactions

Audit Fields

Soft Delete Strategy

Timestamp Consistency

Migration Quality

Seed Data

Slow Queries

Prisma Query Optimization

==========================================================================
PHASE 7 — FRONTEND REVIEW
==========================================================================

Review:

Routing

Layouts

Protected Routes

State Management

Redux

TanStack Query

Forms

Validation

Loading States

Skeletons

Empty States

Error States

Accessibility (WCAG)

Responsive Design

Dark Mode

Code Splitting

Lazy Loading

Performance

Component Reuse

API Layer

==========================================================================
PHASE 8 — BUSINESS MODULE VALIDATION
==========================================================================

Review ALL modules:

Auth

Workers

Employers

Recruiters

Jobs

Applications

Payments

Wallet

Notifications

Messaging

Reviews

AI

Analytics

Admin

Search

Documents

KYC

Referrals

Subscriptions

Knowledge Base

Audit

Health

Validate:

Business Rules

State Machines

Authorization

Transactions

Edge Cases

Concurrency

Race Conditions

Duplicate Prevention

Event Publishing

==========================================================================
PHASE 9 — DEVOPS & INFRASTRUCTURE
==========================================================================

Review:

Docker

Docker Compose

GitHub Actions

Environment Variables

Production Config

Kubernetes

Secrets

Health Checks

Readiness Probes

Liveness Probes

Resource Limits

Logging

Monitoring

Rollback Strategy

Backup Strategy

Recovery Strategy

==========================================================================
PHASE 10 — BUILD VALIDATION
==========================================================================

ACTUALLY EXECUTE:

pnpm install

pnpm lint

pnpm typecheck

pnpm build

pnpm test

pnpm test:coverage

Prisma Generate

Prisma Migration

Docker Compose

If any command fails,

Fix the implementation.

Never bypass failures by weakening linting or compiler rules.

==========================================================================
PHASE 11 — END-TO-END VALIDATION
==========================================================================

Execute and validate:

Worker Registration

Employer Registration

Recruiter Registration

OTP

Login

Refresh Token

Logout

Profile Completion

Create Job

Edit Job

Publish Job

Search Jobs

Apply Job

Withdraw Application

Hire Worker

Notifications

Messaging

Payments

Wallet

Reviews

Admin

Analytics

Verify every workflow against the running application.

==========================================================================
PHASE 12 — PERFORMANCE & OBSERVABILITY
==========================================================================

Validate:

Redis Caching

Kafka Reliability

OpenSearch Indexing

Database Performance

API Latency

Memory Usage

CPU Usage

Bundle Size

OpenTelemetry

Prometheus Metrics

Grafana

Structured Logging

Correlation IDs

Tracing

==========================================================================
FINAL OUTPUT
==========================================================================

Do not stop until all possible validations have been completed.

Provide a complete Engineering Release Report containing:

1. Executive Summary

2. Production Readiness Score (/100)

3. Architecture Score

4. Backend Score

5. Frontend Score

6. Database Score

7. Security Score

8. Performance Score

9. DevOps Score

10. Testing Score

11. Coverage Percentage

12. Build Results

13. Typecheck Results

14. Lint Results

15. Test Results

16. Runtime Validation Results

17. Security Findings

18. Performance Findings

19. Database Findings

20. Infrastructure Findings

21. Critical Issues

22. High Priority Issues

23. Medium Priority Issues

24. Low Priority Issues

25. Files Modified

26. Why Each File Was Modified

27. Remaining Risks

28. Deployment Checklist

29. Rollback Checklist

30. Backup & Recovery Checklist

31. Release Recommendation

The repository may ONLY be declared:

"READY FOR PRODUCTION"

if ALL of the following are TRUE:

✓ Build succeeds

✓ Typecheck succeeds

✓ Lint succeeds

✓ Tests succeed

✓ Coverage targets are met

✓ Prisma migrations execute successfully

✓ Runtime validation succeeds

✓ No Critical Security Issues remain

✓ No Critical Performance Issues remain

✓ No Critical Database Issues remain

✓ No Critical Infrastructure Issues remain

If any requirement cannot be verified because the environment is unavailable, explicitly report it as:

NOT VERIFIED

Never claim success without actual execution.
</USER_REQUEST>
